key_insights.insight: Chasing external markers of success like money doesn't inherently change how you feel about yourself.
key_insights.quote: Every insecurity that I had before the money hit I still have like I feel exactly the same about myself that I did a year ago five years ago.

key_insights.insight: True fulfillment comes from developing skills that are meaningful to you and serve others.
key_insights.quote: Fulfillment is having a set of skills that are very meaningful to you that you worked your ass off to acquire and they don't only serve you, they serve other people.

key_insights.insight: Being a good person and providing value to others is an effective long-term strategy.
key_insights.quote: The number one marketing vehicle is to be a good person and to do amazing things for people and add a crushing amount of value to people's lives.

key_insights.insight: Money should be a secondary driver in decision-making, not the primary one.
key_insights.quote: I'll never again make decisions based on money...Money comes, it's one of the five, but it's the the fifth

key_insights.insight: Your internal sense of self and how you feel when alone is more important than external validation or material wealth.
key_insights.quote: The way that you feel about yourself when you're all alone and it's dark and there's nothing to distract you, how you feel about yourself in that moment is everything.

key_insights.insight: Willingness to do things you don't want to do and to suffer in service of a goal builds your sense of self.
key_insights.quote: What changed something was I showed up at the gym every day. I put in the work. I did things I didn't want to do. I was willing to suffer in service of a goal.

actionable_advice.advice: Identify your top 5 drivers in life, and ensure money is not the primary one.
actionable_advice.how_to: List out the most important things to you. Rank them in order of importance. Make sure purpose, meaning, autonomy, and mastery are all ranked higher than money.

actionable_advice.advice: Develop skills that are valuable to others.
actionable_advice.how_to: Identify areas where you can contribute and improve your skills, focusing on how they benefit others. Seek feedback and continuously refine your abilities to maximize impact.

memorable_stories: The speaker recounts working obsessively for a tech startup, even dictating where his wife could live based on proximity to his work, eventually leading to burnout and re-evaluating his priorities.
The speaker recounts being a 'paper millionaire' but being profoundly unhappy, realizing that money alone couldn't buy happiness.
contrarian_claims: The greatest marketing vehicle of all time is to be a good person.
Money is more powerful than people realize, but not in the way they think; it doesn't change who you are.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 6 - Tom Bilyeu on The TRUE Meaning of Success
